findings.issue: wave field uses string value 'audit' instead of a number. Template specifies `wave: 1` (number ≥ 1).
findings.fix: If the framework supports symbolic wave names (audit / final) — leave as is and add a note to tech-spec / decisions.md. Otherwise replace `wave: audit` with the next numeric wave (e.g. `wave: 5`).

findings.issue: wave field uses string value 'audit' instead of a number.
findings.fix: Same as task 7 — confirm framework accepts symbolic waves or convert to numeric.

findings.issue: wave field uses string value 'audit' instead of a number.
findings.fix: Same as task 7 — confirm framework accepts symbolic waves or convert to numeric.

findings.issue: wave field uses string value 'final' instead of a number.
findings.fix: Same as task 7 — confirm framework accepts symbolic waves or convert to numeric.

findings.issue: Task 9 is an audit task (skills: [test-master], reviewers: []), audit deliverable is a markdown report. Per task-creator rules and the per-task R1 hint in inputs, audit-wave tasks should have verify=[smoke] and no Automated subsection (deliverable is the report). Task 9 includes an `### Automated` subsection running gradle test suites. This is not strictly wrong (auditor needs green tests to audit), but it blurs the line between Test Audit (deliverable: audit report) and Pre-deploy QA (deliverable: gradle pass). Consider moving the gradle commands into ### Smoke as a 'sanity check before audit' step, leaving Automated empty / absent, to align with audit-task convention.
findings.fix: Move `./gradlew :core-runtime:testDebugUnitTest` and `./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest` from Automated to Smoke as preconditions ('audit only proceeds if tests are green'). Or accept current placement — not a critical violation.

findings.issue: Two context links use absolute Windows path (`C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/CLAUDE.md`) and PK paths via `~/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/`. Other tasks in this batch use relative `../../../.claude/skills/...`. Inconsistency: task 6/7 use `~/.claude/skills/...`, task 7/8/9 use `../../../.claude/skills/...`, task 10 mixes both. Not a critical issue (links may resolve correctly in either form), but worth aligning across batch.
findings.fix: Pick one style and apply consistently. Recommended: `~/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/...` (matches template) — works because PK lives at user home, identical for all tasks.

findings.issue: PK path uses `~/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/` for project.md/architecture.md/patterns.md. Tasks 7/8/9 use `../../../.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/`. Both probably resolve, but inconsistency across batch is a smell.
findings.fix: Align PK link style across all tasks in feature. Either all use `~/.claude/...` (template convention) or all use relative `../../../.claude/...`.

findings.issue: Task 8 is audit-wave with verify: [smoke] — correct. But § Verification Steps has no `### User` subsection at all (it does have one in tasks 7 and 9 noting 'Не применимо'). For consistency with other audit tasks in the batch, an explicit User subsection stating it doesn't apply would be clearer. Currently absent — and template says 'Omit section if no user checks', so technically valid.
findings.fix: Optional: add `### User` with 'Не применимо — отчёт audit IS the deliverable' to mirror tasks 7 and 9 style. Or leave absent (per template guidance).

findings.issue: Task 10 inputs note `verify: [smoke, user]` is correct because the task IS the user-verification gate. The Verify-user block is comprehensive and well-structured. Minor: AC-1.4 (non-Ready skip warning log) is verified by unit test, listed under § AC matrix — but AC-1.5 user step asks for `adb logcat` excerpt, which depends on USB-debug being available. Edge case is documented (deferred), good. No fix needed — flagging for awareness.

summary: All 5 tasks (6-10) approved. No critical or major findings. 8 minor stylistic / consistency issues — primarily wave-field-as-string for audit/final waves (4 instances, may be framework convention) and PK-path style inconsistency across batch. Skill / reviewer / verify mapping per inputs is correct: task 6 verify=[smoke,user] delivers UI control, audit tasks 7/8/9 verify=[smoke] correct, task 10 verify=[smoke,user] is the user-verification gate. Carry-forward from tech-spec preserved (Decision 6, engineReady_combinatorics test, AC-2.x). Dependencies correct (wave 4 > wave 3 for task 6, audit > all impl tasks, final > audit). Ready for next phase.
